lede: A look at October 21 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Hongwu Emperor and Almen Abdi, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Canada
events.desc: In Canada, the 2019 Canadian federal election ends, resulting in incumbent Prime Minister Justin Trudeau remaining in office, albeit with the Liberal Party in a minority government.

events.title: Eris (dwarf planet)
events.desc: Images of the dwarf planet Eris are taken and subsequently used in documenting its discovery.

events.title: Agreed Framework
events.desc: North Korea and the United States sign an Agreed Framework that requires North Korea to stop its nuclear weapons program and agree to inspections.

events.title: Honduras
events.desc: In Honduras, 131 people are killed when a Boeing 727 crashes on approach to Toncontín International Airport near the nation's capital Tegucigalpa.

events.title: Solomon R. Guggenheim Museum
events.desc: In New York City, the Solomon R. Guggenheim Museum opens to the public.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Kragujevac massacre against Serbian men and boys takes place.

events.title: HMS Niobe (1897)
events.desc: HMS Niobe arrives in Halifax Harbour to become the first ship of the Royal Canadian Navy.

events.title: Medicine Lodge Treaty
events.desc: The Medicine Lodge Treaty is signed by southern Great Plains Indian leaders. The treaty requires Native American Plains tribes to relocate to a reservation in the western Indian Territory.

events.title: Florence Nightingale
events.desc: Florence Nightingale and a staff of 38 nurses are sent to the Crimean War.

events.title: First Crusade
events.desc: First Crusade: Crusaders led by Godfrey of Bouillon, Bohemund of Taranto, and Raymond IV, Count of Toulouse, begin the Siege of Antioch.

births.name: Alfred Nobel
births.line: Swedish chemist and engineer, invented dynamite and founded the Nobel Prize

births.name: Benjamin Netanyahu
births.line: Israeli captain and politician, 9th Prime Minister of Israel

births.name: Dizzy Gillespie
births.line: American trumpet player, composer, and bandleader

deaths.text: Gough Whitlam, Australian lieutenant, lawyer, and politician, 21st Prime Minister of Australia
Yash Chopra, Indian director, producer, and screenwriter
François Truffaut, French actor, director, producer, and screenwriter

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Charles of Austria (Roman Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: Laura of Saint Catherine of Siena
